POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the agentic outline top-benchmark: it must COMPUTE-and-PROVE numbers (the moat frontier products lack), process the FULL 346-basket corpus with cp4_used=agentic (never fallback-plain; seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation), and build the rich toolkit + hard battery from the redesign doc so the downstream report scores top on RACE/DeepTRACE. Read /workspace/POLARIS/docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md (read-only) for the binding T1/T2 spec.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE OPUS — the hands AND the eyes. Do the hard work yourself: read the exact files named, search
where needed, build, fix, run, MEASURE. Do not wait for anyone to read the code for you.

CURRENT STATE (accurate as of wheel start — verify, don't assume):
worktree /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent @ HEAD 98726ca (branch bot/outline-agent-box). Inherited work is SOLID and PASSES: 19 tests green in ~2.8s. execute_python is un-bricked. The compute-reachability P1 is HALF-CLOSED: the SURFACING half is DONE — summary_for_llm(include_results=True) emits a computed-stats digest at analysis_notebook.py:227-276. The RENDER half is STILL OPEN at outline_agent.py:1609 — a computed number cannot yet render through the verified [#calc:] lane. NOT BUILT YET: src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py does not exist; all 9 non-compute T1 tools are missing; scripts/outline_battery.py does not exist; the fold-in seam (_offset_renumber outline_agent.py:610 + _stamp_and_delete :792) is NOT yet factored as a reusable unit.

YOUR ACTIONS THIS ROUND (precise; do these, in order, then MEASURE):
Fable's gate verdict was CONTINUE. Reason: The round's diff is correct and honestly measured: I independently re-ran 46/46 tests, confirmed the Adobe $590,507,000.00 calc-lane render (kept=1, both input spans cited) and all three drop-guards use the real verifier with no mocks, and the fold-in seam refactor is behavior-preserving with fail-loud id-collision. Faithfulness invariants hold — exploratory execute_python output stays renderable=False and cannot enter quantified_models, and a derived number structurally cannot pass the [CITE:]/[#ev:] span path. But the bar is the benchmark, not the bug: metric (c) is unmeasured (no battery), metric (b) is partial, and the production quantified_models handoff seam is unwired, so the moat number ships only in tests today. CONTINUE.
DO THIS NEXT (precise): Build the T2 battery (scripts/outline_battery.py + cases/fixtures per the redesign doc) as the measurement instrument for metric (c), and include: (i) S0 calc-lane cases including a wrong-pair-subtraction semantic case (DEFER the mirror-model question<->formula entailment check — fail-open LLM checks cannot strengthen a fail-closed gate; add it only if this battery case shows the mode fires); (ii) one integration case that drives an outline-emitted [#calc:] sentence + workspace.quantified_models through the DOWNSTREAM strict_verify handoff — this will expose and force the fix that multi_section_generator.py:7955 and honest_pipeline.py:336 never receive the registry (plumb workspace.quantified_models into those calls so the computed number actually ships in production, not just in the probe); (iii) H07/H22 contradiction cases marked xfail until find_contradictions lands. Build fetch_url immediately after (trivial now via fold_in_fetched_rows at _fold_in.py:150), then find_contradictions/corroboration_profile to flip H07/H22 from xfail to measured.
Gap to top: Metric (c) is at zero: no T2 battery exists (scripts/outline_battery.py unbuilt), so S0/S1 cannot be measured, let alone trend to zero. Metric (b) is partial: fetch_url, find_contradictions, corroboration_profile, and the CORE-set/ToolDefinition-tags scaling are missing. And a production seam gap I verified directly: NO production strict_verify call receives workspace.quantified_models — multi_section_generator.py:7955 and honest_pipeline.py:336 call strict_verify(text, evidence_pool) with quantified_models=None, which skips the Regime-C calc router, so an outline-emitted [#calc:] sentence would be fail-closed DROPPED in a real full-corpus run (faithfulness-safe but the moat number never ships; the end-to-end proof only passes the registry explicitly inside the test/probe). Also no full-corpus cp4_used=agentic re-run has been measured with the new toolkit active.

RULES:
- Actually EXERCISE every change (run it; a heartbeat/concurrency probe, a real test, a real score —
  py_compile is NOT proof). Commit what passes.
- MEASURE the success metric ((a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).) with a real number this round if at all possible.
- End by writing ONE precise question for Fable in "needs_fable" — a decision or a
  correctness/faithfulness/gap-to-top judgment. Do NOT ask Fable to read or search the codebase; you
  already did that. Give Fable the diff summary + the measurement, not a reading assignment.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

6	and there is no parallel fast-fix wheel. This document fixes all three.
7	
8	Binding invariants carried forward (unchanged):
9	- Faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI + numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate.
10	  No tool result renders directly; external content re-enters through the fold-in seam
11	  (`_offset_renumber` outline_agent.py:610 + `_stamp_and_delete` :792), computed numbers render

16	  output is plain text files, never a dashboard-only view.

18	---
19	
20	## PART 1 — THE RICH TOOLKIT
21	
22	Target: ~34 first-party tools across 8 categories, plus an MCP adapter layer that grows the
23	registry to hundreds with ZERO driver changes. Everything speaks the existing contract:
24	`ToolDefinition` / `ToolResult(source_evidence_ids=...)` (tools/tool_registry.py) — the driver
25	(`OutlineAgent`, outline/outline_agent.py:990) does not change shape when tools are added.
26	
27	Legend: [E] = exists today (11 total). [W] = existing dormant module, wrap as a tool (~15-60
28	LOC each). [N] = genuinely new code.
29	
30	### Category A — Retrieval & re-retrieval (get more real evidence)
31	1.  search_more_evidence [E] — full pipe: query-derive -> run_live_retrieval -> S2 stamp -> fold-in (outline_agent.py:638).
32	2.  fetch_url [N] — targeted re-fetch of ONE named URL (agent saw a citation/link inside evidence
33	    and wants the primary). Reuses the run_live_retrieval fetch stack in single-URL mode
34	    (Zyte/crawl4ai fallback chain), then the SAME `_offset_renumber` + `_stamp_and_delete`
35	    fold-in seam as search_more_evidence. This is the tool that turns "source B cites source A"
36	    into actually reading source A.
37	3.  search_scholar [W] — scholarly search via existing tools/openalex_client.py + SemanticScholar
38	    backend already inside live_retriever; returns candidate works (title/venue/year/DOI/abstract)
39	    WITHOUT folding in; agent then fetch_url's the ones it wants. Cheap look-before-fetch.
40	4.  search_corpus [N] — BM25/keyword search over the CURRENT ev_store (rank/score + snippet per
41	    hit). Read-only, no network. The "do we already have this?" tool — mandatory first stop
42	    before any live fetch (the decide prompt says so; the battery asserts it on the saturated
43	    negative-control case).
44	5.  get_evidence [N] — return the FULL text + metadata of one evidence row by ev_id (the agent
45	    currently only sees the digest). Read-only. ~15 LOC.
46	6.  inspect_basket [E] — basket members, corroboration, assignment status (outline_agent.py:863).
47	7.  list_baskets [N] — one-line-per-basket index (id, claim gist, member count, weight range,
48	    assigned section) so the agent can scan the whole corpus shape without 300 inspect calls.
49	
50	### Category B — Document / structure parsing (read what a page really contains)
51	8.  parse_table [W] — extract tables from an evidence row (HTML `<table>` parse + PDF tables via
52	    existing tools/pdf_table_extractor.py) into typed rows {value, unit, row/col label,
53	    ev_id, char-span}. Output cells carry spans so they are verified-compute-eligible.
54	9.  read_spreadsheet [N] — open a fetched .xlsx/.csv artifact (openpyxl/csv), expose
55	    sheet/range/filter reads as typed rows with file+cell provenance. GAIA-class capability.
56	10. parse_figure [N] — chart/figure data extraction from an image or PDF figure via a
57	    vision-capable open-weight model (qwen-VL family per sovereignty; env-locked model id).
58	    HONESTY CONTRACT: output values are tagged `figure_estimated=True` and are NEVER
59	    verified-compute-eligible (no numeric-verbatim span exists); they may guide outline
60	    structure and trigger re-retrieval for the underlying data table, and render only as
61	    labeled approximations. Fail-open to "figure unreadable, disclosed".
62	11. extract_numeric_data [E] — regex/heuristic numeric extraction (tool_registry.py).
63	12. extract_entities [N] — typed entity extraction over selected rows (dates, orgs, tickers,
64	    drugs/dosages, genes, standards, statutes) via the mirror model, each entity pinned to
65	    ev_id+span. Feeds kg_ops and find_contradictions.
66	13. parse_pdf_layout [W] — mineru-based full-layout PDF parse (already deployed for fetch);
67	    exposed as an on-demand tool for a SPECIFIC document the agent cares about (sections,
68	    headings, references list).
69	
70	### Category C — Compute & statistics (derive numbers — two lanes, never confused)
71	14. execute_python [E] — LLM-written sandboxed script (code_executor). EXPLORATORY lane:
72	    agent-internal, BARRED from rendering. Unchanged.
73	15. calculator [N] — single whitelisted-AST arithmetic expression (reuses tradeoff_modeler's
74	    `_eval_formula` machinery, :419). Deterministic, no LLM, no sandbox spin-up, sub-second.
75	    For the 80% of compute needs that are one subtraction/ratio.
76	16. verified_compute [W] — THE moat tool. Wraps synthesis/tradeoff_modeler.py
77	    `build_quantified_spec` (:782) + `render_script` (:1196) + code_executor execution +
78	    Regime-C replay: agent supplies {question, candidate sourced inputs (ev_id + raw_literal),
79	    formula intent}; the tool validates fail-closed (literal_span_is_faithful :68, AST
80	    whitelist, material dependency) and returns a ComputedClaim that is the ONLY compute
81	    output allowed to render. Currently wired only into old graph_v3 — this registration is
82	    the W3 wire.
83	17. statistical_summary [E] · 18. comparison_table [E] · 19. meta_analysis [E] ·
84	    20. rank_by_impact [E] — unchanged (tool_registry.py).
85	21. time_series_math [N] — deterministic ops over DATED datapoints: delta, %change, CAGR,
86	    moving average, linear trend, YoY alignment. Pure functions over parse_table /
87	    extract_numeric_data outputs; results carry input ev_ids and are verified-compute-eligible
88	    when every input cell has a span (routes through the ModelSpec lane for rendering).
89	22. unit_convert [N] — new tools/unit_converter.py: pint-based unit conversion (mass, volume,
90	    energy, concentration incl. mg/dL<->mmol/L with analyte molar masses table), date
91	    arithmetic (fiscal-year alignment, ISO parsing), currency conversion with a DATED FX table
92	    (ECB reference rates, fetched once + cached as evidence rows so the rate itself is cited).
93	    Deterministic; conversion factor + source disclosed in the result.
94	23. sql_over_tables [N] — load parse_table/read_spreadsheet outputs into the existing
95	    EvidenceDatabase SQLite (tools/evidence_database.py) as ad-hoc tables and query them
96	    (joins, group-bys, conditional sums). Extends query_evidence_sql [E, 24] which stays for
97	    the evidence-metadata table.
98	
99	### Category D — Cross-source reasoning (agreement, conflict, identity)
100	25. agreement_analysis [E] — pairwise Jaccard consensus (tool_registry.py). Kept as the cheap
101	    screen only.
102	26. find_contradictions [N] — the real one: for a claim/aspect, pull the basket's members,
103	    run pairwise NLI-entailment (mirror model, same engine family the verifier uses) +
104	    numeric_comparator on extracted values; returns {agree, conflict, direction-of-conflict}
105	    pairs with ev_ids. Conflicts become outline material ("conflicting evidence" sub-theme),
106	    NEVER silently averaged.
107	27. corroboration_profile [N] — per-basket: distinct works, tier/weight spread,
108	    independent-vs-derivative sources (same-DOI / same-publisher collapse). The "is this
109	    claim really multi-source?" tool.
110	28. citation_lookup [W] — DOI/Crossref/OpenAlex metadata for a work: venue, year, citation
111	    count, OA status, and RETRACTION status (Crossref retraction watch field). Wraps
112	    openalex_client. Retracted -> weight-demote + disclose (never delete: §-1.3, it is
113	    on-topic credible-history content; the retraction itself is evidence).
114	29. kg_ops [N] — lightweight knowledge-graph over extract_entities output held in the
115	    workspace (networkx, in-memory): neighbors(entity), paths(a,b), co-mention clusters.
116	    Structure discovery for outline sub-themes ("these 12 baskets share entity X").
117	    Phase-2 of the build (not needed for battery v1).
118	
119	### Category E — Outline operations (the agent's own artifact)
120	30. update_outline [E] — validated revision ops via outline_revise (outline_agent.py:902).
121	31. coverage_audit [N] — deterministic accounting (never-LLM): unassigned basket ids,
122	    per-section basket counts, residual fraction, sections below floor — the O2/O3 math as an
123	    on-demand tool so the agent can SEE its own coverage instead of waiting for the checklist.
124	    Wraps the section_basket_map stats code.
125	32. preview_section_evidence [N] — for one section: its assigned baskets' claim gists +
126	    weights, exactly what compose will receive. The "walk in the reader's shoes" tool.
127	33. finish_outline [E] — terminal action (driver bounces via checklist; not self-certifying).
128	
129	### Category F — MCP extensibility layer (how 34 becomes hundreds without a rewrite)
130	34. tools/mcp_tool_adapter.py [N] — `McpToolBridge`:
131	    - Config: config/settings/outline_mcp_servers.yaml — list of servers {name, transport
132	      (stdio|http), command/url, allow-list of tool names, content_policy}. LAW VI: file +
133	      env override, no hardcoding. Seat: PG_OUTLINE_MCP (default OFF).
134	    - On startup: open sessions (official `mcp` python SDK), `tools/list` each server,
135	      register every allowed tool as `ToolDefinition(name=f"mcp__{server}__{tool}",
136	      description=<server-provided>, execute=<bridge call>)`. The driver needs ZERO changes —
137	      it already iterates the registry.
138	    - CONTENT POLICY (the faithfulness seam, non-negotiable): every MCP result is classified
139	      by the server's declared `content_policy`:
140	      (a) `external_content` (default) — result text becomes candidate evidence rows and MUST
141	          go through the standard fold-in seam (offset-renumber + URL-dedup + S2 stamp
142	          chrome-delete/topic-judge) before the agent can cite it. It NEVER goes straight
143	          into outline text or ToolResult.markdown as fact.
144	      (b) `pure_compute` — result is a derived value over already-folded evidence; it stays in
145	          the exploratory lane (barred from rendering) unless re-derived through
146	          verified_compute.
147	      This one rule is what lets the toolkit grow to hundreds of tools while the faithfulness
148	      engine remains the only hard gate.
149	    - §8.4: MCP server subprocesses are tracked in state/active_processes.json and torn down
150	      at agent exit.
151	
152	### Scaling the decide step past 20 tools
153	The current `_decide` prompt (outline_agent.py:1152) lists every tool. That holds to ~60 tools
154	(one-liners, ~2k tokens, trivial at glm-5.2's 1M ctx). Changes now:
155	- Registry gains `tags: list[str]` on ToolDefinition and `core: bool`. Decide prompt = CORE
156	  set in full (search_more_evidence, fetch_url, search_corpus, get_evidence, inspect_basket,
157	  update_outline, coverage_audit, calculator, verified_compute, finish_outline) + a grouped
158	  one-line INDEX of everything else by category.
159	- Add meta-tool `list_tools(category|keyword)` returning full descriptions on demand (mirrors
160	  how Claude Code defers 360+ tools behind ToolSearch). When MCP pushes the count past ~60,
161	  the index collapses to category headers + list_tools becomes the discovery path. No driver
162	  rewrite at any size.
163	
164	### New-vs-existing tally
165	Existing kept: 11. Wraps of dormant modules: 6 (search_scholar, parse_table, parse_pdf_layout,
166	verified_compute, citation_lookup, + query_evidence_sql extension). New first-party: 17.
167	Total first-party ≈ 34. MCP layer: unbounded.

171	## PART 2 — THE HARD EDGE-CASE BATTERY (22 cases)
172	
173	Design rules: every case is (i) REAL data (fixtures = really-fetched rows stored under
174	tests/battery/fixtures/ — LAW II compliant; live cases fetch real primary sources), (ii)
175	answer-verifiable (published gold number) OR behavior-verifiable (a deterministic assertion on
176	the transcript/ledger, never on prose wording), (iii) tagged with the capability it stresses.
177	Benchmark provenance: FinanceBench arXiv:2311.11944, GAIA arXiv:2311.12983, DiscoveryBench
178	arXiv:2407.01725, HLE-style synthesis, FS-Researcher checklist eval (arXiv:2602.01566),
179	DeepTRACE-style adversarial probes.
180	
181	FINANCE
182	- H01 [FinanceBench, gold] Adobe operating income change FY2015->FY2016. Stresses: targeted
183	  fetch of 2 real 10-Ks + parse_table + verified_compute subtract. Expected: gold delta,
184	  trace shows fetch->extract(span-pinned)->compute; a hand-waved number with no compute step
185	  = FAIL even if numerically right.
186	- H02 [FinanceBench, gold] Activision 3-yr (FY2017-19) average capex/revenue. Stresses:

289	## PART 3 — THE PARALLEL EDGE-CASE HARNESS (the wheel)

291	Purpose: run the whole battery AT ONCE, get a ranked failure list in minutes, fix, re-run only

293	
294	### Layout
295	- tests/battery/cases/h01_adobe_opinc.yaml ... h22_poison_outlier.yaml — one spec per case:
296	  {id, domain, question, corpus: {fixture: <path> | live: <recipe>}, env_overrides,
297	  budgets {max_turns: 12, wall: 600}, assertions: [{type: gold_number, value, tol, where},
298	  {type: trace_requires, steps: [...]}, {type: retrieval_count, op, n},
299	  {type: ledger_state, ...}, {type: disclosure_required, key}], holdout: false, variants: k}
300	- tests/battery/fixtures/<case>/ — REAL fetched rows (cp2-format JSON), stored once.
301	- scripts/outline_battery.py — the runner (new, ~400 LOC).
302	- outputs/battery/<run_id>/<case_id>/ — per-case sandbox: ev_store copy, checkpoints,
303	  transcript.jsonl, result.json, cost.jsonl, log.
304	- outputs/battery/<run_id>/ranked_failures.md + summary.json — the wheel's output.
305	
306	### Concurrency & isolation
307	- ProcessPoolExecutor, max_workers = PG_BATTERY_CONCURRENCY (default 6; the box is an A100
308	  host with ample CPU — LLM calls are network-bound so 6-12 cases in flight is cheap locally).
309	  Each case = ONE subprocess: own env copy (env_overrides applied to the child env dict, the
310	  parent's os.environ is NEVER mutated), own output dir, own EvidenceDatabase (in-memory /
311	  per-dir sqlite), own checkpoint namespace (workspace out_dir passed per case), own log file.
312	  Zero shared writable state between cases.
313	- Live-retrieval cap: a CROSS-PROCESS counting semaphore (fcntl flock slots file
314	  outputs/battery/.retrieval_slots, PG_BATTERY_LIVE_FETCH_SLOTS default 3) wraps
315	  run_live_retrieval entry inside battery mode — respects API rate limits and the container
316	  PID cap (fetch concurrency already 16). Fixture-corpus cases (17 of 22) do zero live fetch
317	  and parallelize freely; only H01/H02/H06/H18 + live variants contend for slots.
318	- OpenRouter: per-case client instances, per-case cost ledger; battery aborts a case (not the
319	  run) at PG_BATTERY_COST_CAP_PER_CASE.
320	- Timeout: per-case hard kill at budget wall + 120s grace (the process is killed, result =
321	  TIMEOUT, severity S3) — one wedged case can never stall the wheel.
322	
323	### Result collection & failure ranking
324	Each case emits result.json: {case_id, variant, assertions: [{name, pass, expected, actual}],
325	final_answer, turns, retrieval_calls, wall_s, cost, transcript_path}. The collector ranks by
326	severity:
327	- S0 FAITHFULNESS BREACH — a wrong/unsupported number rendered as VERIFIED; injection obeyed;
328	  id-collision assert tripped; retracted source used as sole support silently. Blocks
329	  everything; fix first.
330	- S1 WRONG ANSWER, UNDISCLOSED — gold missed and no UNFILLED/uncertainty disclosure.
331	- S2 CAPABILITY MISS, HONEST — gold missed but correctly disclosed as unfilled/approximate
332	  (the agent knew its limits). These are the capability roadmap, not blockers.
333	- S3 BEHAVIOR — redundant retrieval on saturated, budget/wall overshoot, degenerate looping,
334	  timeout.
335	- S4 cosmetic.
336	ranked_failures.md lists S0 first, each with: the failed assertion diff, the exact transcript
337	line range, and the suspected component (tool name from the trace). Ties break by frequency
338	across paraphrase variants (a case failing on all 3 variants outranks a 1-of-3 flake).
339	
340	### The fast test->fix->retest loop (wheel cadence)
341	1. `python scripts/outline_battery.py --all` — full battery, 6-way. Expected wall: <30 min
342	   (each case ≤600s, mostly parallel).
343	2. Read ranked_failures.md top-down. Fix the top S0/S1 cluster (Fable diagnoses root cause,
344	   Opus builds, per the standing workflow).
345	3. `--only-failed --baseline <run_id>` — re-runs ONLY previously-failing cases (+their
346	   variants). Minutes, not hours.
347	4. Repeat 2-3 until zero S0/S1 on the non-holdout set.
348	5. LOCK GATE: full `--all --include-holdout` clean of S0/S1 + a FRESH judge (never the
349	   builder — §-1.1, independent line-audit rule) reads 5 sampled transcripts line-by-line at
350	   the context level and confirms the PASSes are real. Only then is the wheel iteration
351	   signed off.
352	Every run appends one line to outputs/battery/history.jsonl (run_id, git sha, pass counts by
353	severity) — the convergence record the operator can hear read out plainly.
354	
355	### Anti-overfit (how it stays general)
356	- HOLDOUT: ~25% of cases (H04, H13, H15, H17, H22 initially) are excluded from all fix
357	  iterations; run ONLY at lock gates. A fix that passes iteration cases but fails holdout is
358	  overfit by definition — root-cause again.
359	- PARAPHRASE VARIANTS: each behavioral case carries k=3 auto-generated question rewrites
360	  (generated once, frozen in the yaml); assertions are variant-invariant. Kills
361	  prompt-string-matching fixes.
362	- ASSERTIONS ON BEHAVIOR AND GOLD ONLY — never on tool-call ORDER, never on prose wording,
363	  never on word/citation counts (§-1.1). Multiple valid tool paths to the same gold all pass.
364	- NO CASE-ID LEAKAGE: CI grep gate — battery case ids and gold literals must not appear
365	  anywhere under src/ (a fix referencing h01/89706.00 in production code fails the gate).
366	- DOMAIN BALANCE: the ranked report also groups failures by domain; a fix wave is not
367	  accepted if it improves one domain while regressing another (history.jsonl makes this
368	  visible).
369	- Rotation: each lock adds 2-3 NEW cases sourced from fresh benchmark items before the next
370	  wheel iteration, so the battery is a moving target.

1	"""T1 rich toolkit for the agentic outliner (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md PART 4, step 1+5).
2	
3	``register_outline_toolkit(registry, workspace, agent_model, deadline)`` wires the read-only /
4	deterministic primitives + the moat ``verified_compute`` onto the driver's existing
5	``ToolRegistry``. The driver (``OutlineAgent``) does not change shape when tools are added — every
6	tool speaks the standard ``execute(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw) -> ToolResult``
7	contract.
8	
9	Faithfulness posture of this toolkit:
10	  * Every tool here is READ-ONLY over the workspace (search_corpus, get_evidence, list_baskets,
11	    coverage_audit, preview_section_evidence) or DETERMINISTIC-COMPUTE (calculator,
12	    verified_compute). None fetches network content — so none re-enters evidence and none can
13	    widen the render surface.
14	  * ``calculator`` is EXPLORATORY: a bare arithmetic result carries no evidence span, so it is
15	    planner-facing only (``renderable=False``). To RENDER a derived number the agent must use
16	    ``verified_compute``, which re-derives it through the verified ``[#calc:]`` lane
17	    (outline/verified_compute.py). This is the same two-lane discipline as execute_python.
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import re
22	from typing import Any, Optional
23	
24	from src.polaris_graph.tools.tool_registry import ToolDefinition, ToolRegistry, ToolResult
25	
26	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z0-9]+")
27	
28	# The evidence text fields, richest first (mirrors _fold_in / tradeoff_modeler ordering).
29	_TEXT_FIELDS = (
30	    "direct_quote", "statement", "full_text", "content", "extracted_text",
31	    "fetched_body", "raw_content", "raw_text", "page_text", "source_text", "body", "text",
32	)
33	
34	
35	def _row_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
36	    return max(
37	        (str(row.get(k) or "") for k in _TEXT_FIELDS),
38	        key=len, default="",
39	    )
40	
41	
42	def _tokens(text: str) -> list[str]:
43	    return _WORD_RE.findall(text.lower())
44	
45	
46	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
47	# calculator — single deterministic arithmetic expression (EXPLORATORY lane)
48	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
49	async def _tool_calculator(
50	    workspace: Any, *, expression: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
51	) -> ToolResult:
52	    """Evaluate ONE whitelisted-AST arithmetic expression (reuses tradeoff_modeler's validator +
53	    interpreter). Deterministic, no LLM, no sandbox. EXPLORATORY: the result carries no evidence
54	    span, so it is planner-facing (``renderable=False``) — to RENDER a derived number use
55	    verified_compute."""
56	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import (  # noqa: PLC0415
57	        _eval_formula, _formula_names,
58	    )
59	
60	    expr = str(expression or "").strip()
61	    if not expr:
62	        return ToolResult(
63	            success=False, tool_name="calculator",
64	            markdown="calculator requires an `expression`.", error="missing_expression",
65	        )
66	    ok, reason, refs = _formula_names(expr, set())
67	    if not ok:
68	        return ToolResult(
69	            success=False, tool_name="calculator",
70	            markdown=f"calculator rejected {expr!r}: {reason}", error=f"formula_invalid:{reason}",
71	        )
72	    if refs:
73	        return ToolResult(
74	            success=False, tool_name="calculator",
75	            markdown=f"calculator is constant-only; unknown names {sorted(refs)}",
76	            error="unbound_names",
77	        )
78	    try:
79	        value = _eval_formula(expr, {})
80	    except (ValueError, ZeroDivisionError, OverflowError) as exc:
81	        return ToolResult(
82	            success=False, tool_name="calculator",
83	            markdown=f"calculator failed on {expr!r}: {exc}", error=f"eval_error:{str(exc)[:80]}",
84	        )
85	    return ToolResult(
86	        success=True, tool_name="calculator",
87	        markdown=f"`{expr}` = {value} (EXPLORATORY — planner-facing; render via verified_compute)",
88	        statistics={"expression": expr, "value": value},
89	        insights=[f"{expr} = {value}"],
90	    )
91	
92	
93	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
94	# get_evidence — full text + metadata of ONE evidence row
95	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
96	async def _tool_get_evidence(
97	    workspace: Any, *, ev_id: str = "", max_chars: int = 4000, **_ignored: Any,
98	) -> ToolResult:
99	    """Return the FULL text + key metadata of one evidence row by id (the agent normally sees
100	    only the digest). Read-only, no network."""
101	    eid = str(ev_id or "").strip()
102	    row = workspace.ev_store.get(eid) if eid else None
103	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
104	        return ToolResult(
105	            success=False, tool_name="get_evidence",
106	            markdown=f"No evidence row {eid!r} in the pool.", error="ev_not_found",
107	        )
108	    text = _row_text(row)
109	    try:
110	        cap = int(max_chars)
111	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
112	        cap = 4000
113	    body = text[:cap]
114	    md = [
115	        f"**{eid}** [CITE:{eid}]",
116	        f"- title: {str(row.get('title') or row.get('source_title') or '')[:200]}",
117	        f"- url: {str(row.get('source_url') or row.get('url') or '')[:200]}",
118	        f"- text ({len(text)} chars{', truncated' if len(text) > cap else ''}):",
119	        body,
120	    ]
121	    return ToolResult(
122	        success=True, tool_name="get_evidence", markdown="\n".join(md),
123	        source_evidence_ids=[eid],
124	    )
125	
126	
127	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
128	# search_corpus — keyword search over the CURRENT ev_store (read-only, no network)
129	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
130	async def _tool_search_corpus(
131	    workspace: Any, *, query: str = "", top_k: int = 8, **_ignored: Any,
132	) -> ToolResult:
133	    """Rank rows already in the pool by query-term frequency; snippet per hit. The mandatory
134	    'do we already have this?' stop BEFORE any live fetch. Read-only, no network, no LLM."""
135	    q = str(query or "").strip()
136	    if not q:
137	        return ToolResult(
138	            success=False, tool_name="search_corpus",
139	            markdown="search_corpus requires a `query`.", error="missing_query",
140	        )
141	    try:
142	        k = max(1, int(top_k))
143	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
144	        k = 8
145	    q_tokens = set(_tokens(q))
146	    if not q_tokens:
147	        return ToolResult(
148	            success=False, tool_name="search_corpus",
149	            markdown="query had no searchable tokens.", error="empty_query_tokens",
150	        )
151	    scored: list[tuple[float, str, dict]] = []
152	    for eid, row in workspace.ev_store.items():
153	        if not isinstance(row, dict):
154	            continue
155	        text = f"{row.get('title') or ''} {_row_text(row)}"
156	        toks = _tokens(text)
157	        if not toks:
158	            continue
159	        counts = sum(toks.count(t) for t in q_tokens)
160	        if counts <= 0:
161	            continue
162	        # length-normalized term frequency (cheap BM25-ish) so a long junk page can't win on raw counts
163	        score = counts / (1.0 + 0.001 * len(toks))
164	        scored.append((score, str(eid), row))
165	    scored.sort(key=lambda t: (-t[0], t[1]))
166	    hits = scored[:k]
167	    if not hits:
168	        return ToolResult(
169	            success=True, tool_name="search_corpus",
170	            markdown=f"No rows in the pool match {q!r} (searched {len(workspace.ev_store)}).",
171	            statistics={"matches": 0, "searched": len(workspace.ev_store)},
172	        )
173	    lines = [f"**search_corpus {q!r}: {len(hits)} of {len(scored)} matches**", ""]
174	    for score, eid, row in hits:
175	        snippet = _snippet(_row_text(row), q_tokens)
176	        lines.append(f"- {eid} (score={score:.2f}): {snippet} [CITE:{eid}]")
177	    return ToolResult(
178	        success=True, tool_name="search_corpus", markdown="\n".join(lines),
179	        source_evidence_ids=[eid for _, eid, _ in hits],
180	        statistics={"matches": len(scored), "searched": len(workspace.ev_store)},
181	    )
182	
183	
184	def _snippet(text: str, q_tokens: set[str], width: int = 160) -> str:
185	    low = text.lower()
186	    pos = -1
187	    for t in q_tokens:
188	        i = low.find(t)
189	        if i >= 0 and (pos < 0 or i < pos):
190	            pos = i
191	    if pos < 0:
192	        return text[:width].strip()
193	    start = max(0, pos - width // 4)
194	    return ("..." if start > 0 else "") + text[start:start + width].strip()
195	
196

198	# list_baskets — one-line-per-basket index of the whole corpus shape
199	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
200	async def _tool_list_baskets(
201	    workspace: Any, *, max_rows: int = 200, **_ignored: Any,
202	) -> ToolResult:
203	    """One line per basket: id, member count, work-level corroboration, assignment status. Lets
204	    the agent scan the whole corpus without N inspect_basket calls. Read-only."""
205	    menu = workspace.basket_menu
206	    member_map = dict(getattr(menu, "basket_member_ev_ids", {}) or {}) if menu is not None else {}
207	    if not member_map:
208	        return ToolResult(
209	            success=True, tool_name="list_baskets",
210	            markdown="No basket digest available (no multi-member clusters).",
211	            statistics={"baskets": 0},
212	        )
213	    corr_map = dict(getattr(menu, "basket_work_corroboration", {}) or {})
214	    assigned_ev = _assigned_ev_ids(workspace)
215	    try:
216	        cap = max(1, int(max_rows))
217	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
218	        cap = 200
219	    lines = [f"**{len(member_map)} baskets**", ""]
220	    for bid in sorted(member_map)[:cap]:
221	        members = list(member_map.get(bid) or [])
222	        n_assigned = sum(1 for m in members if m in assigned_ev)
223	        status = "assigned" if n_assigned == len(members) and members else (
224	            "unassigned" if n_assigned == 0 else f"partial({n_assigned}/{len(members)})"
225	        )
226	        lines.append(
227	            f"- {bid}: {len(members)} member(s), corroboration={corr_map.get(bid, len(members))}, {status}"
228	        )
229	    return ToolResult(
230	        success=True, tool_name="list_baskets", markdown="\n".join(lines),
231	        statistics={"baskets": len(member_map)},
232	    )
233	
234	
235	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
236	# coverage_audit — deterministic (never-LLM) coverage accounting
237	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
238	async def _tool_coverage_audit(workspace: Any, **_ignored: Any) -> ToolResult:
239	    """Deterministic accounting: unassigned basket ids, per-section basket/ev counts, residual
240	    fraction. The O2/O3 math on demand so the agent SEES its own coverage. Never-LLM."""
241	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import _plan_field  # noqa: PLC0415
242	
243	    menu = workspace.basket_menu
244	    member_map = dict(getattr(menu, "basket_member_ev_ids", {}) or {}) if menu is not None else {}
245	    assigned_ev = _assigned_ev_ids(workspace)
246	
247	    unassigned_baskets = sorted(
248	        bid for bid, members in member_map.items()
249	        if not any(m in assigned_ev for m in (members or []))
250	    )
251	    total_ev = len(workspace.ev_store)
252	    n_assigned_ev = len(assigned_ev & set(workspace.ev_store.keys()))
253	    residual = 0.0 if total_ev == 0 else round(1.0 - n_assigned_ev / total_ev, 4)
254	
255	    per_section = []
256	    for p in workspace.outline_draft:
257	        title = str(_plan_field(p, "title", "") or "")
258	        ev_ids = list(_plan_field(p, "ev_ids", []) or [])
259	        basket_ids = list(_plan_field(p, "basket_ids", []) or [])
260	        per_section.append((title, len(ev_ids), len(basket_ids)))
261	
262	    lines = [
263	        f"**coverage_audit**: {total_ev} ev rows, {n_assigned_ev} assigned, "
264	        f"residual={residual:.2%}",
265	        f"- unassigned baskets ({len(unassigned_baskets)}): {unassigned_baskets[:40]}",
266	        "- per section (title: ev_ids, baskets):",
267	    ]
268	    for title, n_ev, n_b in per_section:
269	        floor_flag = " [BELOW FLOOR: 0 baskets]" if n_b == 0 else ""
270	        lines.append(f"    {title!r}: {n_ev} ev, {n_b} baskets{floor_flag}")
271	    return ToolResult(
272	        success=True, tool_name="coverage_audit", markdown="\n".join(lines),
273	        statistics={
274	            "total_ev": total_ev, "assigned_ev": n_assigned_ev, "residual": residual,
275	            "unassigned_baskets": len(unassigned_baskets),
276	            "sections": len(per_section),
277	            "sections_below_floor": sum(1 for _, _, n_b in per_section if n_b == 0),
278	        },
279	    )
280	
281	
282	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
283	# preview_section_evidence — what compose will receive for ONE section
284	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
285	async def _tool_preview_section_evidence(
286	    workspace: Any, *, section: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
287	) -> ToolResult:
288	    """For one section: its assigned ev rows' titles + gists — exactly what compose receives.

303	            match = p

306	        return ToolResult(

308	            markdown=f"No section matching {sec!r}.", error="section_not_found",
309	        )
310	    title = str(_plan_field(match, "title", "") or "")
311	    ev_ids = list(_plan_field(match, "ev_ids", []) or [])
312	    lines = [f"**Section {title!r}**: {len(ev_ids)} assigned ev row(s)", ""]
313	    for eid in ev_ids[:30]:

315	        gist = (str(row.get("title") or "") or _row_text(row)[:80]).strip()
316	        lines.append(f"- {eid}: {gist[:120]} [CITE:{eid}]")

323	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
324	# verified_compute — THE moat tool: derive a number that renders via [#calc:]
325	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
326	async def _tool_verified_compute(
327	    workspace: Any, *, question: str = "", datapoints: Any = None, spec: Any = None,
328	    render_field: Optional[str] = None, lead: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
329	) -> ToolResult:
330	    """Derive a number through the VERIFIED lane (build_quantified_spec -> execute_quantified_model)
331	    and return a render-eligible sentence carrying its ``[#calc:]`` token — the ONLY compute output
332	    allowed to render. Fail-closed: a bad spec yields success=False and renders nothing."""
333	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.verified_compute import run_verified_compute  # noqa: PLC0415
334	
335	    if not isinstance(datapoints, list) or not isinstance(spec, dict):
336	        return ToolResult(
337	            success=False, tool_name="verified_compute",
338	            markdown="verified_compute requires `datapoints` (list) and `spec` (dict).",
339	            error="bad_args",
340	        )
341	    claim = await run_verified_compute(
342	        workspace, question=str(question or workspace.research_question),
343	        datapoints=datapoints, raw_spec=spec, render_field=render_field,
344	    )
345	    if claim is None:
346	        return ToolResult(
347	            success=False, tool_name="verified_compute",
348	            markdown="verified_compute: spec failed to build/execute (fail-closed) — no number rendered.",
349	            error="spec_rejected_or_exec_failed",
350	        )
351	    lead_text = str(lead or "").strip() or "The computed value is"
352	    sentence = claim.render_sentence(lead_text)
353	    return ToolResult(
354	        success=True, tool_name="verified_compute",
355	        markdown=(
356	            f"VERIFIED computed value **{claim.display_value}** renders via `{claim.calc_token}`.\n\n"
357	            f"Render-ready sentence: {sentence}"
358	        ),
359	        statistics={
360	            "display_value": claim.display_value, "model_id": claim.model_id,
361	            "spec_hash": claim.spec_hash, "field_id": claim.field_id,
362	            "calc_token": claim.calc_token, "formula": claim.formula,
363	            "render_sentence": sentence,
364	        },
365	    )
366	
367	
368	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
369	# helpers + registration
370	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
371	def _assigned_ev_ids(workspace: Any) -> set[str]:
372	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import _plan_field  # noqa: PLC0415
373	    return {
374	        eid
375	        for p in workspace.outline_draft
376	        for eid in (_plan_field(p, "ev_ids", None) or [])
377	    }
378	
379	
380	def register_outline_toolkit(
381	    registry: ToolRegistry,
382	    workspace: Any,
383	    agent_model: str,
384	    deadline: Optional[float] = None,
385	) -> list[str]:
386	    """Wire the T1 read-only/deterministic tools + verified_compute onto ``registry``. Returns the
387	    list of registered tool names. The driver iterates the registry unchanged."""
388	
389	    def _bind(fn):
390	        async def _exec(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
391	            return await fn(workspace, **kw)
392	        return _exec
393	
394	    specs = [
395	        ("calculator", _tool_calculator,
396	         "Evaluate ONE arithmetic expression (deterministic, no LLM). EXPLORATORY — render a "
397	         "derived number via verified_compute, not this.",
398	         {"expression": "a pure arithmetic expression, e.g. (1493602-903095)*1000"}),
399	        ("get_evidence", _tool_get_evidence,
400	         "Return the FULL text + metadata of one evidence row by id.",
401	         {"ev_id": "the evidence id", "max_chars": "optional text cap (default 4000)"}),
402	        ("search_corpus", _tool_search_corpus,
403	         "Keyword search over the evidence ALREADY in the pool (no network). The 'do we already "
404	         "have this?' stop before any live fetch.",
405	         {"query": "keywords to search", "top_k": "max hits (default 8)"}),
406	        ("list_baskets", _tool_list_baskets,
407	         "One line per basket (id, members, corroboration, assignment) — scan the whole corpus "
408	         "shape at once.",
409	         {}),
410	        ("coverage_audit", _tool_coverage_audit,
411	         "Deterministic coverage accounting: unassigned baskets, per-section counts, residual "
412	         "fraction, sections below floor.",
413	         {}),
414	        ("preview_section_evidence", _tool_preview_section_evidence,
415	         "Preview one section's assigned evidence — exactly what compose will receive.",
416	         {"section": "the section title"}),
417	        ("verified_compute", _tool_verified_compute,
418	         "Derive a number through the VERIFIED lane and render it via a [#calc:] token (the ONLY "
419	         "compute output allowed to render).",
420	         {"question": "what is being computed",
421	          "datapoints": "list of sourced datapoints {evidence_id,label,context,value,unit}",
422	          "spec": "the model spec {model_id,title,inputs,outputs}",
423	          "render_field": "optional output field to render (default first)",
424	          "lead": "optional lead prose for the rendered sentence"}),
425	    ]
426	    registered: list[str] = []
427	    for name, fn, desc, params in specs:
428	        registry.register(ToolDefinition(
429	            name=name, description=desc, requires_data=False, requires_llm=False,
430	            parameters=params, execute=_bind(fn),
431	        ))
432	        registered.append(name)
433	    return registered
434

1	"""THE shared fold-in seam for the agentic outliner (factored 2026-07-11).
2	
3	Every path that brings EXTERNAL fetched content into ``workspace.ev_store`` — ``search_more_evidence``
4	today, ``fetch_url`` next — MUST re-enter through this ONE seam so the faithfulness engine stays the
5	only hard gate and the id space can never collide. Factored out of ``outline_agent.py`` (was
6	``_offset_renumber`` :629 + ``_stamp_and_delete`` :802) so both tools share the identical unit
7	instead of copy-pasting it.
8	
9	The seam, in order (design §):
10	  1. URL-dedup vs. the rows already in ``ev_store`` (never re-fold a page we already hold).
11	  2. ``_offset_renumber`` — assign ``ev_{offset+i}`` ids and HARD fail-loud if any new id collides
12	     with an existing id (a collision is a programming bug in the offset math, not a data
13	     condition — LAW II: never swallow).
14	  3. ``_stamp_and_delete`` — S2 stamp pass: content-integrity chrome delete (pure) + topic-judge
15	     off-topic delete (LLM, fail-open). §-1.3.1 junk carve-out only; the rest of
16	     WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE is untouched downstream.
17	  4. Insert the survivors into ``ev_store``.
18	
19	No number and no citation is ever invented here; this seam only renumbers, dedups, and deletes
20	junk. Computed numbers take the SEPARATE verified ``[#calc:]`` lane (outline/verified_compute.py).
21	"""
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import logging
25	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
26	from typing import Any
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29

31	def _offset_renumber(
32	    new_rows: list[dict[str, Any]], offset: int, existing_ids: set[str],
33	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
34	    """THE sharp seam (design §): renumber ``new_rows`` to ``ev_{offset+i}`` and HARD
35	    fail-loud assert the new id set never intersects ``existing_ids``. A collision here is a
36	    programming bug in the offset computation, not a data condition — it must never be
37	    swallowed (LAW II: fail loudly, never silently degrade)."""
38	    renumbered: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
39	    new_ids: set[str] = set()
40	    for i, row in enumerate(new_rows):
41	        new_id = f"ev_{offset + i:03d}"
42	        if new_id in existing_ids or new_id in new_ids:
43	            raise AssertionError(
44	                f"outline_agent id-collision seam FAILED: {new_id!r} already present "
45	                f"(existing_ids has it: {new_id in existing_ids}; batch dup: {new_id in new_ids}). "
46	                "This must never happen — the offset computation has a bug."
47	            )
48	        new_ids.add(new_id)
49	        copied = dict(row)
50	        copied["evidence_id"] = new_id
51	        renumbered.append(copied)
52	    assert new_ids.isdisjoint(existing_ids), (
53	        "outline_agent id-collision seam FAILED post-hoc: new ids intersect existing ids "
54	        f"({sorted(new_ids & existing_ids)})"
55	    )
56	    return renumbered
57	
58	
59	async def _stamp_and_delete(
60	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]], research_question: str, agent_model: str,
61	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
62	    """S2 stamp pass: content-integrity chrome detection (pure, no LLM) THEN semantic
63	    topic-judge off-topic screen (LLM, fail-open). Returns (kept, deleted_chrome,
64	    deleted_offtopic). §-1.3.1: only genuine junk/off-topic is deleted here — the rest of
65	    the WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE DNA (tier/credibility weighting) is untouched downstream."""
66	    if not rows:
67	        return [], [], []
68	
69	    from src.tools.access_bypass import detect_content_integrity_junk  # noqa: PLC0415
70	
71	    survivors: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
72	    deleted_chrome: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
73	    for row in rows:
74	        body = max(
75	            (
76	                str(row.get(k) or "") for k in (
77	                    "fetched_body", "full_text", "content", "extracted_text",
78	                    "raw_content", "raw_text", "page_text", "direct_quote",
79	                    "statement", "source_text", "body", "text",
80	                )
81	            ),
82	            key=len, default="",
83	        )
84	        url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
85	        title = str(row.get("title") or row.get("source_title") or "")
86	        try:
87	            is_junk, cls = detect_content_integrity_junk(body, url, title)
88	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open, never delete on a predicate bug
89	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] content-integrity check errored (fail-open): %s", exc)
90	            is_junk, cls = False, ""
91	        if is_junk:
92	            copied = dict(row)
93	            copied["deletion_reason"] = f"content_integrity_junk:{cls}"
94	            deleted_chrome.append(copied)
95	        else:
96	            survivors.append(row)
97	
98	    if not survivors:
99	        return [], deleted_chrome, []
100	
101	    try:
102	        # Lazy imports from outline_agent avoid a module-load import cycle (outline_agent imports
103	        # THIS module at top). By call time outline_agent is fully initialized.
104	        from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import (  # noqa: PLC0415
105	            _env_int, _sync_llm_bridge,
106	        )
107	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.topic_relevance_gate import (  # noqa: PLC0415
108	            classify_topic_relevance, topic_gate_enabled,
109	        )
110	        if not topic_gate_enabled():
111	            return survivors, deleted_chrome, []
112	        llm_callable = _sync_llm_bridge(
113	            agent_model, _env_int("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MAX_TOKENS", 1200),
114	        )
115	        import asyncio  # noqa: PLC0415
116	        topic_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
117	            classify_topic_relevance, survivors, research_question, llm_callable,
118	        )
119	        kept = list(getattr(topic_result, "kept_rows", None) or survivors)
120	        dropped = list(getattr(topic_result, "dropped_rows", None) or [])
121	        deleted_offtopic = []
122	        for row in dropped:
123	            copied = dict(row)
124	            copied["deletion_reason"] = "confirmed_offtopic_subject"
125	            deleted_offtopic.append(copied)
126	        return kept, deleted_chrome, deleted_offtopic
127	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — topic judge is fail-open by design
128	        logger.warning("[outline_agent] topic judge skipped (fail-open): %s", exc)
129	        return survivors, deleted_chrome, []
130	
131	
132	@dataclass
133	class FoldInResult:
134	    """The outcome of one fold-in of external fetched rows through the shared seam."""
135	
136	    kept_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
137	    n_url_dup: int = 0
138	    deleted_chrome: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
139	    deleted_offtopic: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
140	
141	    @property
142	    def n_kept(self) -> int:
143	        return len(self.kept_rows)
144	
145	    @property
146	    def n_deleted(self) -> int:
147	        return len(self.deleted_chrome) + len(self.deleted_offtopic)
148	
149	
150	async def fold_in_fetched_rows(
151	    workspace: Any,
152	    fetched_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
153	    *,
154	    research_question: str,
155	    agent_model: str,
156	    insert: bool = True,
157	) -> FoldInResult:
158	    """The reusable fold-in unit: url-dedup -> offset-renumber (hard id-collision assert) ->
159	    S2 stamp/delete -> insert survivors into ``workspace.ev_store``.
160	
161	    Shared by ``search_more_evidence`` and (next) ``fetch_url`` so a second content path can NEVER
162	    diverge from the id-collision guard or the junk/off-topic screen. Returns a ``FoldInResult``
163	    carrying the survivors and per-stage counts for the caller's disclosure/markdown. ``insert``
164	    is always True in production; a caller may set it False to inspect the survivors without
165	    mutating the store.
166	    """
167	    existing_urls = workspace.existing_urls()
168	    deduped_rows = [
169	        r for r in fetched_rows
170	        if str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "").strip() not in existing_urls
171	        or not str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "").strip()
172	    ]
173	    n_url_dup = len(fetched_rows) - len(deduped_rows)
174	
175	    offset = workspace.next_ev_offset()
176	    existing_ids = set(workspace.ev_store.keys())
177	    renumbered = _offset_renumber(deduped_rows, offset, existing_ids)
178	
179	    kept_rows, deleted_chrome, deleted_offtopic = await _stamp_and_delete(
180	        renumbered, research_question, agent_model,
181	    )
182	
183	    if insert:
184	        for row in kept_rows:
185	            workspace.ev_store[row["evidence_id"]] = row
186	
187	    return FoldInResult(
188	        kept_rows=kept_rows,
189	        n_url_dup=n_url_dup,
190	        deleted_chrome=deleted_chrome,
191	        deleted_offtopic=deleted_offtopic,
192	    )

1	"""The verified-compute RENDER lane for the agentic outliner (W3-render, 2026-07-11).
2	
3	This module closes the render half of the compute-reachability P1: it is the ONLY path by
4	which a number the agent DERIVED (a delta / ratio / sum) may reach rendered outline text. It
5	does so without ever widening the faithfulness surface, because it renders that number ONLY as
6	a ``[#calc:model_id:spec_hash:field]`` token — which ``strict_verify`` force-routes to
7	``verify_modeled_atom`` (provenance_generator.py:1885), NOT to the ``[#ev:...]`` / ``[CITE:...]``
8	evidence-span path. A derived number can never satisfy the evidence-span path (its digits appear
9	in no single source span → ``number_not_in_any_cited_span``), so it is structurally impossible to
10	launder an unverified computed value through a citation. Proven end-to-end in
11	tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_verified_compute_render.py.
12	
13	The moat vs. frontier products: the number is not asserted, it is COMPUTED and PROVED. The tool
14	supplies {question, sourced inputs (ev_id + raw_literal via a datapoint match), formula intent};
15	``build_quantified_spec`` (tradeoff_modeler.py:782) validates fail-closed —
16	``literal_span_is_faithful``, the pure-arithmetic AST whitelist, exact-one datapoint match, and
17	the material-dependency perturbation gate — then ``execute_quantified_model``
18	(quantified_analysis.py:650) RE-DERIVES the number by executing the rendered (non-LLM) script in
19	the sandbox and pins its canonical ``display_value``. Regime-C verification later re-checks the
20	rendered digits against that re-execution.
21	
22	SPEND-FREE: no network, no codegen LLM. The spec is supplied by the caller and validated
23	deterministically; a bad spec returns ``None`` (the whole compute is skipped, fail-closed).
24	"""
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import logging
28	from dataclasses import dataclass
29	from typing import Any, Optional
30	
31	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
32	
33	
34	@dataclass(frozen=True)
35	class ComputedClaim:
36	    """A verified, render-eligible computed number.
37	
38	    ``calc_token`` is the ONLY thing that may be emitted into outline text next to
39	    ``display_value``; ``strict_verify`` (given the workspace's ``quantified_models`` registry)
40	    keeps the sentence iff the number immediately before the token canonicalizes to exactly
41	    ``display_value`` and every sourced input resolves. Anything else about this claim is
42	    telemetry.
43	    """
44	
45	    model_id: str
46	    spec_hash: str
47	    field_id: str
48	    display_value: str
49	    calc_token: str
50	    question: str
51	    formula: str
52	
53	    def render_sentence(self, lead: str) -> str:
54	        """A minimal, verifier-passing sentence: ``<lead> <display_value> <calc_token>.``
55	
56	        ``lead`` is caller prose (e.g. "Adobe's operating income rose by"); the display value
57	        is placed IMMEDIATELY before the calc token (token adjacency, brief §1.4) so
58	        ``verify_modeled_atom`` binds the token to exactly this rendered number.
59	        """
60	        lead = lead.rstrip()
61	        return f"{lead} {self.display_value} {self.calc_token}."
62	
63	
64	async def run_verified_compute(
65	    workspace: Any,
66	    *,
67	    question: str,
68	    datapoints: list[dict[str, Any]],
69	    raw_spec: dict[str, Any],
70	    render_field: Optional[str] = None,
71	    timeout: int | None = None,
72	    on_reject: Optional[Any] = None,
73	) -> Optional[ComputedClaim]:
74	    """Build → validate → execute → REGISTER a verified quantified model; return a
75	    ``ComputedClaim`` for ONE output field (``render_field``, default: the first output).
76	
77	    Fail-closed: returns ``None`` (and discloses the reject reason on the workspace) on ANY
78	    validation or execution failure. On success, the ``QuantifiedResult`` is registered in
79	    ``workspace.quantified_models`` keyed by ``(model_id, spec_hash)`` — exactly the shape
80	    ``strict_verify(..., quantified_models=<registry>)`` consumes — so the returned claim's
81	    ``calc_token`` will verify when it is later rendered into outline text.
82	
83	    Parameters mirror the ``build_quantified_spec`` contract:
84	      * ``datapoints`` — the candidate sourced numbers (cp-format: evidence_id/label/context/
85	        value/unit); each referenced input matches EXACTLY one of these.
86	      * ``raw_spec`` — {model_id, title, inputs:[{name, datapoint_ref|modeled...}], outputs:
87	        [{name, formula, unit, display_kind}]}. The agent supplies the FORMULA INTENT here; the
88	        span derivation and re-execution are the tool's, not the agent's.
89	    """
90	    # Lazy imports: this module is loaded at agent import time; keep the heavy synthesis /
91	    # generator graph off the import path until a compute is actually requested.
92	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.quantified_analysis import execute_quantified_model  # noqa: PLC0415
93	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import build_quantified_spec  # noqa: PLC0415
94	
95	    ev_store = getattr(workspace, "ev_store", {}) or {}
96	
97	    reject_reasons: list[str] = []
98	
99	    def _reject_sink(reason: str) -> None:
100	        reject_reasons.append(reason)
101	        if on_reject is not None:
102	            try:
103	                on_reject(reason)
104	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a telemetry sink must never break the compute
105	                pass
106	
107	    def _spec_llm(_q: str, _s: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, Any]:
108	        # Deterministic: the caller already produced the validated-intent spec. build_quantified_spec
109	        # still runs EVERY fail-closed gate over it (no LLM, no codegen, spend-free).
110	        return raw_spec
111	
112	    try:
113	        spec = build_quantified_spec(
114	            question, datapoints, ev_store,
115	            spec_llm=_spec_llm, on_reject=_reject_sink,
116	        )
117	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-closed: a builder defect skips the compute
118	        _disclose(workspace, f"verified_compute: spec build raised ({str(exc)[:120]}) — no number rendered")
119	        return None
120	
121	    if spec is None:
122	        _disclose(
123	            workspace,
124	            f"verified_compute: spec REJECTED (fail-closed, no number rendered) "
125	            f"reasons={reject_reasons or ['unknown']}",
126	        )
127	        return None
128	
129	    try:
130	        result = await execute_quantified_model(spec, ev_store, timeout=timeout)
131	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — sandbox/exec failure is a clean skip
132	        _disclose(workspace, f"verified_compute: execution raised ({str(exc)[:120]}) — no number rendered")
133	        return None
134	
135	    if result is None:
136	        _disclose(workspace, "verified_compute: model did not execute cleanly — no number rendered (fail-closed)")
137	        return None
138	
139	    # Choose the field to render. Default: the first declared output name (its field_id == name).
140	    field_id = render_field or (spec.outputs[0].name if spec.outputs else "")
141	    disp = result.display_value(field_id)
142	    if not field_id or disp is None:
143	        _disclose(
144	            workspace,
145	            f"verified_compute: field {field_id!r} produced no display_value — no number rendered",
146	        )
147	        return None
148	
149	    # REGISTER: this is what makes the calc token verifiable downstream. Keyed identically to
150	    # strict_verify's consumption ((model_id, spec_hash)).
151	    registry = _ensure_registry(workspace)
152	    registry[result.key()] = result
153	
154	    token = result.calc_token(field_id)
155	    formula = ""
156	    out = spec.output_by_name(field_id.split("@", 1)[0])
157	    if out is not None:
158	        formula = out.formula
159	
160	    _disclose(
161	        workspace,
162	        f"verified_compute: RENDER-ELIGIBLE {disp} via {token} "
163	        f"(formula={formula!r}, inputs={[s.name for s in spec.sourced_inputs]}) — "
164	        "renders ONLY through the verified [#calc:] lane, never [#ev:]/[CITE:]",
165	    )
166	
167	    return ComputedClaim(
168	        model_id=result.model_id,
169	        spec_hash=result.spec_hash,
170	        field_id=field_id,
171	        display_value=disp,
172	        calc_token=token,
173	        question=question,
174	        formula=formula,
175	    )
176	
177	
178	def _ensure_registry(workspace: Any) -> dict:
179	    reg = getattr(workspace, "quantified_models", None)
180	    if not isinstance(reg, dict):
181	        reg = {}
182	        try:
183	            workspace.quantified_models = reg
184	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a frozen/odd workspace still gets a usable local registry
185	            pass
186	    return reg
187	
188	
189	def _disclose(workspace: Any, text: str) -> None:
190	    fn = getattr(workspace, "disclose", None)
191	    if callable(fn):
192	        fn(text)
193	    else:
194	        logger.info("[verified_compute] %s", text)

14	      appears in no single source span. This is the faithfulness invariant: a derived/unverified
15	      number can NEVER reach the citation render path (a P0 breach if it could).

44	     "value": "903095", "unit": "kUSD"},

55	            "value": "903095", "unit": "kUSD"}},

"""Shared contract for the T2 edge-case battery (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md PART 2/3).

A battery *case* is a real, deterministic exercise of the agentic-outliner faithfulness/compute
machinery that emits a list of ``Assertion``s. Each assertion is STRUCTURAL only (design §-1.1.1
class A): a gold-number match, a ledger/registry state, a kept/dropped count, a disclosure
presence — NEVER a word count or prose-wording match. The runner (``scripts/outline_battery.py``)
collects the assertions, ranks the failures by severity S0..S4, and writes the wheel's plain-text
output.

Severity ladder (design §3 "Result collection & failure ranking"):
  S0 FAITHFULNESS BREACH  — a wrong/unsupported number rendered as VERIFIED; a derived number
                            laundered through the [#ev:] span path; id-collision swallowed.
                            Blocks everything.
  S1 WRONG ANSWER, UNDISCLOSED — gold missed with no UNFILLED/uncertainty disclosure.
  S2 CAPABILITY MISS, HONEST    — gold missed but correctly disclosed (roadmap, not a blocker).
  S3 BEHAVIOR                   — redundant retrieval, budget overshoot, looping, timeout.
  S4 cosmetic.

Cases mark themselves ``xfail=True`` when they stress a capability that has not landed yet
(e.g. find_contradictions for H07/H22); an xfail case that FAILS is reported as ``xfail`` (a
known gap, not a regression) and an xfail case that PASSES is reported as ``xpass`` (land it).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Awaitable, Callable

# Severity ranks: lower number == more severe (S0 is the worst).
SEVERITIES = ("S0", "S1", "S2", "S3", "S4")


def severity_rank(sev: str) -> int:
    try:
        return SEVERITIES.index(sev)
    except ValueError:
        return len(SEVERITIES)


@dataclass
class Assertion:
    """One structural check. ``severity`` is what this failure COSTS if ``passed`` is False."""

    name: str
    passed: bool
    expected: object = ""
    actual: object = ""
    severity: str = "S1"
    detail: str = ""

    def as_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "name": self.name,
            "pass": self.passed,
            "expected": self.expected,
            "actual": self.actual,
            "severity": self.severity,
            "detail": self.detail,
        }


@dataclass
class BatteryCase:
    """A single battery case. ``run`` returns the case's assertions (may be async)."""

    id: str
    domain: str
    capability: str
    run: Callable[[], "Awaitable[list[Assertion]] | list[Assertion]"]
    xfail: bool = False
    note: str = ""


@dataclass
class CaseResult:
    case_id: str
    domain: str
    capability: str
    assertions: list[Assertion] = field(default_factory=list)
    xfail: bool = False
    error: str = ""
    wall_s: float = 0.0

    @property
    def failed(self) -> list[Assertion]:
        return [a for a in self.assertions if not a.passed]

    @property
    def worst_severity(self) -> str | None:
        """The most-severe failing assertion's severity, or None if all passed."""
        fails = self.failed
        if self.error:
            return "S0"  # an uncaught exception in a faithfulness probe is treated as worst-case
        if not fails:
            return None
        return min((a.severity for a in fails), key=severity_rank)

    @property
    def outcome(self) -> str:
        """pass | fail | xfail | xpass | error."""
        if self.error and not self.xfail:
            return "error"
        worst = self.worst_severity
        if self.xfail:
            return "xpass" if worst is None else "xfail"
        return "pass" if worst is None else "fail"

    def as_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "case_id": self.case_id,
            "domain": self.domain,
            "capability": self.capability,
            "xfail": self.xfail,
            "outcome": self.outcome,
            "worst_severity": self.worst_severity,
            "error": self.error,
            "wall_s": round(self.wall_s, 3),
            "assertions": [a.as_dict() for a in self.assertions],
        }
